Nurse_Dialogue_One: Have you noticed any fluctuations in your weight recently?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: I did. It's strange, because I've [not been eating more than usual, but I'm still gaining weight.]
Dialogue_Ending: I see. I'll note that down real quick.
Symptoms: Potential weight gain from fluid rentention

Nurse_Dialogue_One: Are you feeling any abdominal pain? Or were you feeling any abdominal pain recently?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: None that I noticed.
Dialogue_Ending: Alright, I'll note that down.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: I can see here on your chart that you've have a [history of Stage-2 Hypertension]?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: I do. I've been dealing with it for 2 years.
Dialogue_Ending: I see. Thank you for telling me.
Symptoms: Has a history of Stage-2 Hypertension.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: How have you been feeling lately?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: ... To be honest with you, psychiatrist told me I'm showing some symptoms of [depression] lately. I suppose that might be how I'm feeling.
Dialogue_Ending: I'm really sorry to hear that. I appreciate you feeling comfortable enough to share this with me.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: Have you ever had any tests or checks done on your heart?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: I did. They said my heart isn't pumping enough blood effectively.
Dialogue_Ending: I see…so a [history of heart-failure induced injection fraction], to be specific.
Symptoms: Has a history of heart-failure induced injection fraction

Nurse_Dialogue_One: How frequently have you been taking your medications?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: To be honest with you, I [skip the water pill] sometimes cause it makes me pee too much... Usually I'm [too tired to get up.]
Dialogue_Ending: I see. Thanks for being honest. Will note that down...
Symptoms: Skips the water pill, Chronic Exhuastion

Nurse_Dialogue_One: When you urinate, do you feel like you have to go immediately?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: No. Not really.
Dialogue_Ending: So [no urinary urgency]…that's good.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: Do you feel feverish?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: Uhhh...no.
Dialogue_Ending: Alright, that's good to know.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: I noticed on your chart that you've been dealing with [SOB for the past four days]. How has it been affecting your day-to-day?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: It's been *breathe* bad for *breathe* the past 4 days. I can't *breathe* even shower, *breathe*, or go to the kitchen *breathe* to make food.
Dialogue_Ending: I'm sorry to hear that. Let me just note that down.
Symptoms: Has been dealing with SOB for the past four days.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: It sounds like you have a cough right now.
Patient_Dialogue_Two: Yeah. It's a pretty dry cough, though.
Dialogue_Ending: A dry cough? That means it's [raspy and non-productive].

Nurse_Dialogue_One: It says here you've been taking Digoxin.
Patient_Dialogue_Two: Yes I am. My doctor said [Digoxin is helpful for my heart condition].
Dialogue_Ending: You're right. It increases the strength of heart contractions.
Symptoms: Taking Digoxin for his heart condition

Nurse_Dialogue_One: It looks like you've been [prescribed Furosemide]?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: The water pill? Yes...
Dialogue_Ending: Good to know. It's a diuretic and we may have to check your glucose levels/eletrolytes if that's the case.
Symptoms: Taking Furosemide to mitigate water retention, check for glucose/electrolyte levels

Nurse_Dialogue_One: I can see on your chart that you're [taking Spironolactone].
Patient_Dialogue_Two: That's a water pill, right?
Dialogue_Ending: Yes. Spironolactone is a diuretic that helps with water retention.
Symptoms: Taking Spironolactone to mitigate water retention.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: I just wanted to check, but it says here you're also taking Lisinopril?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: Yes. I think it has to do with my heart condition?
Dialogue_Ending: That's right. [Lisinopril helps open your blood vessels], as a matter of fact.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: It also says here on the chart that you're [taking Sertraline].
Patient_Dialogue_Two: It's to help with my depression...but what does that have to do with my heart condition?
Dialogue_Ending: I just wanted to check.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: Were you also [prescribed Carvedilol]? Are you taking it regularly?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: I am. I take it because I have high blood pressure.
Dialogue_Ending: I see. Especially with your medical history, it's a preventative maintenance for heart failure.
Symptoms: SYSTEMS PLS CHECK THIS BC THIS MED IS NOT ON THE HF SLIDES

Nurse_Dialogue_One: It looks like you listed Colace as a medication you're taking regularly.
Patient_Dialogue_Two: Yes, it helps me with my constipation.
Dialogue_Ending: I'll note that down.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: Based on your SOB, it might be better to recline slightly. Would you like me to assist you?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: Oh, yes. That would be great.
Dialogue_Ending: *animation of [reclining patient/bed 30 degrees]*
Symptoms: Assisted patient into a recline position (30 degrees)

Nurse_Dialogue_One: I noticed that you're [breathing more with your mouth]...
Patient_Dialogue_Two: Yes. It's easier/less tiring for me to breathe this way.
Dialogue_Ending: We might have to check your oxygen levels then. Just so we can cover all our bases.
Symptoms: Shortness of Breath, Check Oxygen Levels

Nurse_Dialogue_One: Are you currently working?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: No, I'm retired FDNY Engine 205, Ladder 118. Stayed with those boys through some more difficult years...
Dialogue_Ending: Thank you for your service, I'll note that down.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: What sort of food do you find yourself eating lately?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: Well, ever since my wife died I've mostly been eating microwave dinners before bed...
Dialogue_Ending: I see, I'm sorry for your loss, just wanted to check.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: Do you have any family in the area?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: Just my two sons, but they moved to the west coast a few years ago.

Nurse_Dialogue_One: What brings you in today?
Patient_Dialogue_Two: I've had some trouble breathing, I'm worried something might be up with my medications...
Dialogue_Ending: Thanks for letting me know, I'll note that down...
